title: Video Title
description: Use a concrete, viewer-facing title instead of an internal note.
recommended: Name the promised outcome or hook.
tip: A good title improves library scanning later.

title: Creative Brief
description: Give the system enough context to build scenes, narration, and visual tone.
recommended: Include audience, angle, and payoff.
tip: If you already know the narration, move it to the advanced narration field.

title: Voice Workflow
description: Choose the narration pipeline that matches latency and voice quality needs.
recommended: Use local for fast drafts, cloud for final polish.
tip: RunningHub voice workflows may benefit from selecting a stronger instance type.

title: Media Workflow
description: This controls the visual rendering strategy for the storyboard and final video.
recommended: Pick the simplest workflow that still fits the motion style.
tip: Changing this has a bigger runtime impact than changing BGM.

title: Background Music
description: Leave empty to keep style defaults or no music, depending on the selected style.
recommended: Only override when the default track clearly mismatches the brief.
tip: Custom BGM is best for branded or campaign-specific output.

title: Custom Narration
description: Overrides topic-based script generation and uses your text verbatim.
recommended: Use this when timing or wording must stay fixed.
tip: If this field is set, the request switches to fixed narration mode.

title: Prompt Prefix
description: Adds a reusable visual style cue before image prompt generation.
recommended: Keep it focused on mood, lens, and composition.
tip: Shorter prefixes are easier to combine with style defaults.

title: Portrait Source
description: Use a stable, front-facing portrait with clean lighting and little motion blur.
recommended: Square or portrait crops usually work best.
tip: Busy backgrounds make lip sync and compositing less reliable.

title: Narration
description: Keep sentences short enough for natural speech pacing and facial sync.
recommended: Write the script as spoken language, not as on-screen copy.
tip: Comma-heavy text often sounds slower than expected.

title: Voice Workflow
description: Optional voice synthesis override for presenter videos.
recommended: Leave empty when the default voice stack is already validated.
tip: Cloud voice workflows can increase queue time.

title: Music Mode
description: Choose whether the presenter clip should ship silent or with a selected track.
recommended: Keep silent for talking-head reviews, add music for publish-ready drafts.
tip: Music can make QA harder when checking lip sync.

title: Music Track
description: Only used when custom music mode is selected.
recommended: Choose low-density tracks that do not compete with speech.
tip: Sparse intros are safer when narration starts immediately.

title: Instance Type
description: Relevant only when the selected voice workflow runs on RunningHub.
recommended: Use auto for drafts, plus for heavy or premium workflows.
tip: Higher tiers improve reliability more than raw speed for bursty queues.

title: Source Image
description: Use a clean still frame with clear subject separation and intentional framing.
recommended: High-contrast edges produce cleaner motion.
tip: Tiny source images amplify artifacts after motion synthesis.

title: Motion Prompt
description: Describe camera movement, subject motion, and emotional pacing in one pass.
recommended: Mention both motion direction and intensity.
tip: Subtle motion is easier to stabilize than large body movement.

title: Motion Workflow
description: Determines how aggressively the system turns still imagery into motion.
recommended: Pick a workflow tuned for the target shot type.
tip: Changing workflows matters more than changing the wording of the prompt.

title: Music Mode
description: Choose whether the resulting clip should stay silent or include a selected track.
recommended: Stay silent when evaluating motion quality.
tip: Music is easier to add later than motion artifacts are to remove.

title: Music Track
description: Only used when custom music mode is enabled.
recommended: Match beat density to the speed of the requested motion.
tip: Fast tracks make slow motion clips feel flat.

title: Instance Type
description: Only shown for RunningHub workflows that need remote GPU capacity.
recommended: Auto is usually enough for exploratory shots.
tip: Reserve plus for heavier pipelines or when queues are unstable.

title: Driver Video
description: Use a clip with clear body motion and minimal occlusion.
recommended: Medium shots with stable framing transfer best.
tip: Fast cuts or handheld shake reduce motion consistency.

title: Target Image
description: Choose a clean subject image that can accept the transferred pose sequence.
recommended: Front-facing, well-lit portraits are the safest baseline.
tip: Complex hair edges and props raise cleanup cost.

title: Pose Workflow
description: Controls how motion is extracted from the driver and applied to the target.
recommended: Use the dedicated pose workflow when motion fidelity matters.
tip: Wrong workflow choice is the fastest path to jitter and broken limbs.

title: Music Mode
description: Decides whether the motion clip is silent or backed by a selected track.
recommended: Keep silent during pose QA.
tip: Audio tends to hide visual problems in quick reviews.

title: Music Track
description: Only used when custom BGM is selected.
recommended: Choose tracks with steady rhythm to match repeated motion.
tip: Overly dramatic builds can overpower short transfer clips.

title: Instance Type
description: Appears for RunningHub-based pose workflows.
recommended: Default to auto unless the workflow is known to be memory-heavy.
tip: Plus is mostly useful for larger or more complex remote pipelines.

title: Scene Media
description: Each scene needs a valid image or video source that matches the narration beat.
recommended: Keep consecutive scenes visually distinct.
tip: Mixed image/video scenes are fine as long as durations stay intentional.

title: Scene Narration
description: Write narration per scene, not a full script block for the entire video.
recommended: Keep one idea per scene.
tip: Shorter scene narration makes pacing easier to debug.

title: Scene Duration
description: Controls how long each scene stays on screen before the next cut.
recommended: Start around 4-6 seconds for narration-led scenes.
tip: Very short durations often clip narration tails.

title: Music Mode
description: Choose whether the stitched custom sequence should ship silent or with a track.
recommended: Use music only after scene timing feels stable.
tip: Silence makes per-scene pacing issues easier to hear.

title: Music Track
description: Optional track for the stitched video.
recommended: Pick tracks that can survive many hard cuts.
tip: Tracks with gentle intros and outros are easier to loop under multiple scenes.
